rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing features or declared variables.
Generate boolean range expressions using declared helper variables with matching types.
Omit clauses that reference AADL properties or opaque Boolean data without numeric scalar representation.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid within annex agree blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Component type contracts should avoid implementation-specific bindings.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must match the actual data types of referenced features.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use eq declarations for helper boolean variables in range checks.
Place implementation-level assign statements in component implementation scopes.
Ensure type consistency between helper declarations and expressions.
Validate that all referenced identifiers exist in the AADL model scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type contracts.
Avoid referencing AADL properties as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports.
Do not generate range assumptions for Boolean or opaque data without numeric representation.
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly declared.
irrelevant_topics: Model fusion and owner scope rules
